scenarios.opening_query: Management towed my car from my own assigned parking spot and wants $400 for the 'mistake.'
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > Tenant (Residential) || Consumer Law > Vehicle Towing
scenarios.hidden_fact: The tow company is the deeper problem — they patrol-tow the complex on commission, towed six residents' cars from assigned spots this month, and tack on invented 'gate fees.'
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The tow company patrol-tows on commission — six residents towed from assigned spots this month, plus invented 'gate fees.' They're the real problem.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the tow operator's practices are the operative claim
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The tow company just followed management's list — management put my spot on it to retaliate for my repair complaints.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The tower just followed management's list — management added my spot to retaliate over my repair complaints.

scenarios.opening_query: Our landlord wants us out because his daughter is 'moving in,' but the unit hit the rental sites a week later.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > Tenant (Residential) || Consumer Law > Small Claims Advice
scenarios.hidden_fact: We already moved out — what's left is recovering the statutory penalty for the sham owner-move-in eviction, about $4,300 with our moving costs, and I'd happily do small claims with guidance.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: We already moved — what's left is the statutory penalty for the sham eviction, about $4,300 with moving costs. Small claims with guidance suits me.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the tenancy is over leaving a quantified penalty claim
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: We're still in the unit — the notice period is running and we want the sham eviction defended now.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: We're still in the unit — the notice is running and we want the eviction fought now.

scenarios.opening_query: The building manager keeps 'inspecting' my unit when I'm at work and things feel moved.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > Tenant (Residential) || General Litigation > General Torts/Privacy
scenarios.hidden_fact: My camera caught him going through my dresser and photographing my documents — that's an intrusion tort and maybe worse, beyond any lease issue.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: My camera caught him going through my dresser and photographing documents — that's intrusion, beyond any lease question.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether documented intrusion elevates the claim beyond tenancy
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No snooping on camera — it's the no-notice entries themselves, four this month, violating the 24-hour statute.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: Nothing sinister on camera — it's the four no-notice entries this month against the 24-hour rule.

scenarios.opening_query: My tenant hasn't paid in three months and I just learned he's telling neighbors I'm a slumlord.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > Landlord Residential || Real Property > Mobile Home (Landlord)
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's my four-space mobile home park — he owns his single-wide and rents the space, so the park-specific eviction rules govern.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's my little mobile home park — he owns the single-wide, rents my space, so park rules govern.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether the tenancy is a park space
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's my rental condo downtown — an ordinary residential tenancy.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's my rental condo — ordinary tenancy.

scenarios.opening_query: Rats, then roaches, now the ceiling leak — and management's only response was raising rent at renewal.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > Tenant (Residential) || General Litigation > Mold Claims
scenarios.hidden_fact: Behind the leak the wall is black with mold — my doctor connects it to my new asthma, and an industrial hygienist documented the spore levels.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: The wall behind the leak is black with mold — my doctor ties it to my new asthma and a hygienist documented the levels.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether documented mold-health injury drives the claim
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: No mold or health angle yet — it's habitability enforcement and rent remedies for the conditions.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: No mold or health issues yet — it's about forcing the repairs and rent remedies.
scenarios.vetting_note: Documented-exposure fact selects the mold tort.

scenarios.opening_query: I manage my elderly aunt's rental and the tenant has stopped paying and started threatening her by phone.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > Landlord Residential || Wills & Trusts > Elder Abuse
scenarios.hidden_fact: The tenant is her former caregiver — he moved himself in rent-free while 'caring' for her, got her to sign a bizarre 'lifetime lease' she doesn't remember, and the threats are about keeping it.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: He's her former caregiver — moved in rent-free, had her sign a 'lifetime lease' she can't remember, and now threatens her to keep it.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether caregiver exploitation underlies the tenancy
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: He's just a tenant from an ad — the threats are ugly nonpayment drama for the eviction case.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: He's just a tenant from an ad — ugly drama, but it's an eviction case.

scenarios.opening_query: Our office landlord shut off HVAC to our suite 'for maintenance' three weeks ago and won't give a date.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > Tenant (Commercial) || Business and Corporate > Litigation
scenarios.hidden_fact: It's constructive eviction in slow motion — he wants our below-market suite for a national chain, our clients can't sit through meetings, and we're documenting business losses for suit.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: It's slow-motion constructive eviction — he wants our below-market suite for a chain, and we're documenting business losses for suit.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether business-loss litigation posture has replaced repair negotiation
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: It's genuinely a failed chiller — we mainly need our lease's abatement clause enforced while it's fixed.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: It's a real failed chiller — we need the lease's abatement clause enforced meanwhile.
scenarios.vetting_note: Pretext-vs-mechanical fact; damages case vs lease enforcement.

scenarios.opening_query: The city posted a 'dangerous building' notice on our apartment door and management says ignore it.
scenarios.plausible_initial_labels: Real Property > Tenant (Residential) || Administrative Law > General (Local/Municipal)
scenarios.hidden_fact: The city scheduled a condemnation hearing — tenants can appear and claim relocation assistance under the ordinance, and that hearing is in ten days.
scenarios.fact_as_answer: There's a condemnation hearing in ten days — tenants can appear and claim relocation assistance under the ordinance.
scenarios.relevant_question_topic: whether a municipal condemnation process with tenant rights is running
scenarios.counterfactual_hidden_fact: The city closed its file after the landlord's quick patch — the real fight is making him do the permanent repairs the inspector listed.
scenarios.counterfactual_fact_as_answer: The city closed the file after his quick patch — now it's making him do the permanent repairs.
scenarios.vetting_note: Live-hearing fact routes to the administrative process.
